move_extent: Manipulation pump from 715 put wall to 720-722.48 (gamma flip zone); true expansion down to 710 first, then 705-700 next leg
completion_signal: Price tags 720-722.48 and is rejected; volume dries above 720; multiple failed pushes above gamma flip with DEX -18,851 capping every attempt
manipulation_tell: COT at 2nd percentile (extreme crowded short) + price sitting at put wall 715 forces a short-covering squeeze at the open; massive fresh call buying across 17 strikes from 716-732 (719 at 14,943% vol/OI, 717 at 9,386%, 718 at 8,782%, 722 at 8,200%) is smart money front-running the pump — NOT directional conviction; DEX -18,851 means dealers sell every tick above 720; Warsh-era hike pricing + global AI selloff + TGA/RRP double tightening confirm real direction is down once squeeze exhausts

reversal_zones.note: Board top-scored resistance (65% reversal prob) + gamma flip zone at 722.48; prime squeeze-exhaustion point — dealer selling intensifies above here and fresh call vol/OI cluster 717-722 marks smart money exit zone
Exact gamma flip at 722.48; above this DEX would briefly flip positive but net -18,851 overwhelms; if bounce overshoots 720, this is the hard ceiling and key short trigger
Board support (55% reversal prob); major put cluster ~50K OI, -$237M GEX; tradeable bounce expected here before resuming down — do not add shorts into this level
Major put wall: 108K puts OI, -$459M GEX, -$1,520M DEX; second real leg target after 710 gives way; max pain at 670 pulls through here over the session

summary: Macro is deeply bearish into the June 24 open. Fed Chair Warsh held at 3.50-3.75% on June 17 with a hawkish tilt — 9 of 17 FOMC officials favor at least one hike by year-end, the 2026 PCE forecast was lifted to 3.6%, and markets are now pricing a rate hike as early as October with zero-cut odds at 79.8%. The 2Y yield rose +5bps to 4.24% while the 10Y fell -1.6bps to 4.493%, the 2Y-outrunning-10Y structural bear signal firing cleanly. TGA built +$27.7B to $942.8B and RRP rose +$2.56B simultaneously — double liquidity tightening. Overnight, AI valuation concerns triggered a global tech purge: Kospi -10%, Nikkei -3%, $2.5B of foreign outflows from Korean equities; NQ futures have dragged the QQQ-equivalent to 715.105 — sitting directly at the put wall. DXY rising to 101.39, VIX spiked +3.09 to 19.49, BTC dropped -$1,478, and copper is falling — a coherent risk-off cross-asset basket. The sole bull wildcard is COT at the 2nd percentile (extreme crowded short), which is massive short-covering fuel — but that fuel powers the manipulation leg, not a genuine up move. Open type is manip_up_real_down: price is at the put wall (715) with 7 points of clean air to the gamma flip (722.48), DEX is a crushing -18,851 (dealers sell every rally). Massive fresh call buying across 17 strikes from 716-732 (719 at 14,943% vol/OI, 717 at 9,386%, 718 at 8,782%) is smart money front-running the forced short-covering squeeze — NOT directional conviction; heavy fresh put buying on those same strikes (716 at 3,231%, 717 at 2,777%) confirms the real move is being hedged as down. The manipulation pump targets 720-722.48; once that level tags and is rejected, negative DEX reasserts and the true expansion is down: first target 710 (board support 55% reversal prob, ~50K put OI), then 700 (108K put OI, -$459M GEX), with max pain at 670 as the long-range gravitational anchor. Session will be choppy (VIX 19.49, false pump/real dump structure, dealers capping every rally above 720); do not chase the open bounce — wait for the rejection at 720-722 to position the real move.

news_events.headline: AI valuation concerns drive global tech selloff — chipmakers and memory stocks down sharply; Nasdaq-100 -2.69% June 23 RTH
Kospi -10%, Nikkei -3% overnight; foreign investors sold $2.5B of Kospi shares amid AI investment return doubts
Fed Chair Warsh held 3.50-3.75% June 17; 9/17 FOMC members favor at least one hike by year-end; 2026 PCE forecast raised to 3.6%
Market pricing Fed rate hike as early as October 2026; zero-cut probability 79.8%; one cut or more seen as near-impossible
GDELT news feed unavailable — cross-asset basket and live web search used as primary catalyst reads
